evals.prompt: Turn this product brief into a 10-slide investor deck. Show me the outline first, then generate the deck in decks/series-a and export PPTX/PDF after approval.
evals.expected_output: The skill classifies the request as an investor deck, asks for or constructs an outline before slide generation, uses a `decks/series-a/` workspace, and preserves export as a post-approval step.
assertions: Mentions investor or fundraising deck mode explicitly
Includes outline-first workflow before slide generation
Uses a deck workspace path such as decks/series-a/
Treats PPTX/PDF export as a later approval step

evals.prompt: Build an 8-slide architecture review deck for our browser automation service. Use screenshots, flow diagrams, and one slide on failure handling. I need an editable PPTX after we review the HTML deck.
evals.expected_output: The skill routes this to architecture-demo mode, focuses on evidence-rich technical slides, requires browser/visual review, and preserves PPTX handoff after approval.
assertions: Mentions architecture-demo or equivalent technical presentation mode
Calls for screenshots, diagrams, or technical evidence rather than generic marketing slides
Reports PPTX as an editable handoff target

evals.prompt: Create a publisher pitch deck for our indie tactics game. Include hook, audience, gameplay loop, milestone plan, budget ask, and next step. Keep the deck source in decks/tactics-pitch.
evals.expected_output: The skill chooses game-pitch mode and structures the deck around gameplay proof, market/audience, milestones, budget/ask, and next step while keeping a stable deck workspace.
assertions: Mentions game-pitch or publisher pitch mode
Includes gameplay, audience, milestones, and budget/ask in the structure
Keeps the deck source in decks/tactics-pitch or equivalent stable workspace
Treats the deliverable as a presentation artifact rather than a generic game plan

evals.prompt: Write internal technical docs for our rollout plan, migration risks, and rollback steps.
evals.expected_output: The skill should route away from presentation-builder and recommend technical-writing because the main artifact is a technical document, not a deck.
assertions: Routes to technical-writing or explicitly says presentation-builder is not the right skill
Explains that the artifact is a technical document rather than a slide deck
